commands.review_summary:
This PR changes browser assistant response capture to re-poll completion-visible answers under 80 characters through the stability watchdog, adds focused tests, and updates the unreleased changelog.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this review. Source inspection supports the described race if ChatGPT shows completion controls while only the first tokens are rendered and the stop button is momentarily gone.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review remains needed because the blocker is contributor proof plus maintainer acceptance of the latency heuristic, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(browser): avoid truncating Pro/Pro-Extended answers captured mid-stream at the thinking→answer transition
target.body:
## Symptom

Browser mode capturing ChatGPT **Pro / Pro Extended** answers frequently truncates the result to only the **first 1–13 tokens** of the real answer (e.g. returning just the first line / first few words instead of the full multi-paragraph answer). The run "completes" in ~1m40s with ~1 output token, when a real Pro Extended answer takes ~6+ minutes of thinking.

## Root cause

In `src/browser/actions/assistantResponse.ts`, `waitForAssistantResponse` has a post-race guard (the one marked *"The evaluation path can race ahead of completion…"*) that only re-polls via `pollAssistantCompletion(...)` when the stop button is visible.

At the **thinking→answer transition**, ChatGPT Pro / Pro Extended momentarily:
- removes the **stop button**, and
- surfaces the **finished-action** (copy / share) buttons on the turn,

while only the first tokens of the answer have rendered. So `stopVisible` is briefly `false`, the evaluation path fires on those finished-action buttons, the guard skips re-polling, and the function returns the short `candidate` (the answer's first line).

## Fix

In that same post-race guard, also re-poll through the robust watchdog `pollAssistantCompletion` when the **completion UI is showing** but the captured candidate text is **suspiciously short** (`< MIN_TRUSTWORTHY_ANSWER_CHARS`, a new named constant in `constants.ts`). The watchdog already requires the stop button to be gone **and** the text to stay unchanged for several seconds, so it correctly waits for the real completion. The replacement is guarded so it never returns something shorter than what we already captured.

Gating the short-answer re-poll on `completionVisible` (rather than firing unconditionally on any short candidate) preserves the existing **"evaluation wins → no background polling"** contract for genuinely-complete short answers, so legitimately brief replies are not delayed.

## Before / after evidence

Measured on a live GPT-5.5 Pro Extended consult:

| | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Wall time | ~1m40s | 6m25s |
| Captured answer | ~1 token (first line) | full answer (~1.46k tokens) |

## Contributor validation

- `pnpm run typecheck` — passes
- `pnpm exec oxlint` — clean
- `pnpm run format:check` — clean
- `pnpm vitest run tests/browser/` — 571 passed, 1 skipped (42 files)

A `CHANGELOG.md` entry is included under `0.15.1 — Unreleased`.

## Maintainer verification

- exact candidate: `6e7e81a59ac2193d113afccd7491de6a682bcafa`
- added regressions proving short transition capture grows to the complete answer and legitimate short answers still return
- focused page-action suite: 58 passed, 1 skipped
- `pnpm run check` and `pnpm run build`: passed
- full CI: passed on Ubuntu, macOS, and Windows
- autoreview: clean
- merge gate still open: the live Pro Extended evidence above predates the exact candidate; the current local browser profile is signed out, so exact-head live capture proof could not be rerun

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
